entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Cultivated peanut (Arachis hypogaea), an allotetraploid crop with a genome size of approximately 2.7 Gb

entities.name: ALLOTETRAPLOID
entities.description: A genomic state where an organism has four sets of chromosomes derived from two different species

entities.name: SUBGENOME
entities.description: The distinct genomic components (A and B) within the allotetraploid peanut genome

entities.name: ARACHIS DURANENSIS
entities.description: A diploid progenitor of the cultivated peanut contributing the A-genome

entities.name: ARACHIS IPAENSIS
entities.description: A diploid progenitor of the cultivated peanut contributing the B-genome

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of the diploid ancestors to understand the peanut genome

entities.name: IPGI
entities.description: International Peanut Genome Initiative, a project that sequenced both A- and B-genomes of peanut ancestors

entities.name: DPPAGSC
entities.description: Diploid Progenitor Peanut Arachis Genome Sequencing Consortium, a project that sequenced the A-genome of peanut ancestors

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE GENES
entities.description: Candidate genes identified using diploid genome data to improve peanut resistance

entities.name: TRANSCRIPT ASSEMBLIES
entities.description: Tetraploid transcript assemblies guided by the sequencing of diploid progenitor genomes

entities.name: GENETIC EXCHANGE
entities.description: The process of genetic material movement between the A and B subgenomes of cultivated peanut

entities.name: GEOCARPY
entities.description: The phenomenon where the peanut fruit develops underground, analyzed via the DPPAGSC study

entities.name: OIL BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: The biological process of producing oils in peanut, analyzed via the DPPAGSC study

entities.name: ALLERGENS
entities.description: Proteins in peanut that cause allergic reactions, analyzed via the DPPAGSC study

entities.name: EVOLUTION
entities.description: The evolutionary history and process of peanut development

entities.name: POLYPLOIDIZATION
entities.description: The process by which the peanut genome acquired multiple sets of chromosomes

entities.name: GENOME ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The structural organization of the complex peanut genome

entities.name: APPLIED GENOMICS
entities.description: The application of genomic information to improve peanut crops

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The science of changing peanut traits to produce desired characteristics
